answer:
We are summing \(\binom{\binom{n}{2}}{2}\) for \(n = 3\) to \(40\).  
First, \(\binom{n}{2} = \frac{n(n-1)}{2}\), then  
\[
\binom{\binom{n}{2}}{2} = \frac{\frac{n(n-1)}{2} \left( \frac{n(n-1)}{2} - 1 \right)}{2}
= \frac{n(n-1)(n(n-1)-2)}{8}.
\]  
This simplifies to \(\frac{n(n-1)(n^2 - n - 2)}{8} = \frac{n(n-1)(n-2)(n+1)}{8}\).  

So the sum is  
\[
S = \sum_{n=3}^{40} \frac{(n-2)(n-1)n(n+1)}{8}.
\]  
We can write \((n-2)(n-1)n(n+1
